300.prompt: Start with this detail: Progress bars reduce uncertainty. Which idea gives the best comparison?
wrongAnswers: Skeleton screens and placebo progress
Progress as measurement problem
Moral and social progress
300.visibleConnection: The question starts from a concrete detail in Progress bars: Progress bars reduce uncertainty. It is really asking which answer handles a similar pressure. Determinate vs indeterminate indicators is the best connection because it separates measurable progress from mere activity, forcing users to decide whether they are seeing information or reassurance. Both ideas ask alpacas to notice that People are calmer when waiting has shape. The danger is that shape can feel like evidence even when the system is guessing or stalling.
300.sourceNote: Level 300 retained for later review; 100/200 answer-choice pass did not rewrite this row.
300.sectionInferenceNote: Direct from V9.1 final CSV source file.

300.prompt: Which idea gives the clearest comparison to Skeleton screens and placebo progress?
wrongAnswers: Moral and social progress
Entropy, maintenance, and regress
Personal progress is non-linear
300.visibleConnection: The question starts from a concrete detail in Skeleton screens and placebo progress: Skeleton screens preview layout before content arrives. It is really asking which answer handles a similar pressure. Progress as measurement problem is the best connection because it turns progress into a choice about value: the metric does not just report the goal, it quietly defines it. Both ideas ask alpacas to notice that People tolerate uncertainty better when a system gives them something to look at or press. The ethical line appears when comfort becomes staged control.
300.sourceNote: Level 300 retained for later review; 100/200 answer-choice pass did not rewrite this row.

300.prompt: Which idea turns the same pressure behind Moral and social progress into a different form?
wrongAnswers: Personal progress is non-linear
Progress bars
Determinate vs indeterminate indicators
300.visibleConnection: The question starts from a concrete detail in Moral and social progress: Rights can expand over time. It is really asking which answer handles a similar pressure. Entropy, maintenance, and regress is the best connection because it makes maintenance the hidden condition of progress; without upkeep, systems slide backward without needing a dramatic collapse. Both ideas ask alpacas to notice that Progress stories can inspire action, but they can also hide who is still excluded after victory has been announced.

300.prompt: Which idea gives the clearest comparison for Personal progress is non-linear?
wrongAnswers: Determinate vs indeterminate indicators
Skeleton screens and placebo progress
Progress as measurement problem
300.visibleConnection: The question starts from a concrete detail in Personal progress is non-linear: Plateaus are not failure. It is really asking which answer handles a similar pressure. Progress bars is the best connection because it turns invisible processing into a visible promise that completion is approaching, even when the user cannot verify real progress. Both ideas ask alpacas to notice that Visible improvement is easy to reward. Invisible consolidation, recovery, and plateau are harder to see but often more real.

300.prompt: Which idea carries the same logic as Determinate vs indeterminate indicators?
wrongAnswers: Entropy, maintenance, and regress
Personal progress is non-linear
Progress bars
300.visibleConnection: The question starts from a concrete detail in Determinate vs indeterminate indicators: Determinate indicators have a visible percentage or fixed path. It is really asking which answer handles a similar pressure. Moral and social progress is the best connection because it tests the belief that history improves by revealing backlash, uneven benefit, and progress that can be declared too early. Both ideas ask alpacas to notice that Users want honesty and comfort at the same time. Interface design often has to choose which kind of trust it is building.
